[notes]
In this case, we use predicates that depend on global information
like we would do for a symbol table. We simply execute
the predicates assuming that all necessary information is available.
The i++ action is done outside of the prediction and so it is executed.

[type]
Parser

[grammar]
grammar T;
@parser::members {i = 0}
s : ({self.i += 1
print("i=",end='',file=self._output)
print(self.i, file=self._output)} a)+ ;
a : {self.i % 2 == 0}? ID {print("alt 1", file=self._output)}
  | {self.i % 2 != 0}? ID {print("alt 2", file=self._output)}
  ;
ID : 'a'..'z'+ ;
INT : '0'..'9'+;
WS : (' '|'\n') -> skip ;

[start]
s

[input]
x x y

[output]
i=1
alt 2
i=2
alt 1
i=3
alt 2
